title: One good turn deserves another
visual_consistency: Recurring Tony Steele: an adult man in his late 30s with neat black hair, a confident friendly smile, lean build, a navy suit, white shirt, and small wallet or coins used sparingly. Recurring narrator: a thoughtful adult with round glasses, short brown hair, average build, a grey jacket, and a mildly surprised comic expression. Keep the same warm restaurant table, simple dinner setting, small bill, and light social humor. For the career-background scene, blend office and bank cues into one continuous setting rather than a side-by-side split layout. Use one single uninterrupted cinematic illustration per frame, no comic panels, no split screen, no collage, no readable text.

frames.prompt: A natural restaurant dinner scene: a thoughtful narrator sits at a table eating dinner while Tony Steele enters the restaurant and notices him. The mood is ordinary and social, with the beginning of a small comic situation. Keep it modern enough for a child to understand but not flashy. No text, no letters, no captions, no readable menus, no readable signs, no visible words.

A simple visual contrast of Tony Steele's background: on one side a calm lawyer office atmosphere from years ago, on the other side a present-day bank workplace feeling. Show Tony as the same adult moving from one work world to another, with a good salary implied by neat professional surroundings. No readable signs, no labels, no money amounts, no text, no letters, no visible words.

Tony repeatedly borrowing small amounts from friends and not returning them: friends look mildly frustrated around a cafe or casual table while Tony casually accepts coins or a small bill. Keep the humour light and social, not hostile, greedy, or mean. Avoid large piles of cash. No text, no letters, no captions, no readable bills, no visible words.

The restaurant twist: the narrator asks Tony for money, Tony immediately hands over a small amount, then confidently points toward the dinner bill while the narrator looks surprised. Make the direction of the joke clear through expressions and gestures, but do not show readable text or numbers on the bill. No text, no letters, no captions, no readable signs, no visible words.
